surfaces.note: sidebar (#0E1220) and card (#12203A) are dark blue. background (#16141D) and input (#221E34) remain dark purple-near-black. Luminance ordering preserved: sidebar < background < card < input. card and input are very close in luminance (0.01467 vs 0.01517) — intentional; they occupy the same visual tier.

text.primary.wcag: AA 4.5:1 normal text — all surfaces pass with very high margin

text.secondary.wcag: AA 4.5:1 normal text — all surfaces pass with very strong margin
text.secondary.note: v4 change: text.secondary shifts from hot pink #FF6EB4 to electric neon blue #3DC8FF. Model names and thread titles are the most frequently recurring chromatic text elements — moving them to the tertiary blue is the highest-impact single redistribution. Hot pink retains its structural dominance at borders.default (card borders); the electric blue claims the textual identity layer. Distinctly bluer than the teal family (#2EE4B9, #6BBFB8) — sits between cyan and sky blue on the spectrum, clearly a different hue.

text.muted.wcag: AA 4.5:1 normal text — all surfaces pass with strong margin
text.muted.note: Desaturated teal — timestamps and placeholder text. Unchanged from v3. Lower luminance than both borders.strong (#2EE4B9) and the new text.secondary (#3DC8FF) — reads as dim/secondary, not competing.

text.inverse.wcag: AA 4.5:1 — passes on all accent-colored button and badge surfaces including the new tertiary blue

borders.default.wcag: borders.default is a structural border (hot pink card edges), not text. WCAG SC 1.4.3 applies to text and images of text only. The 4.40:1 on card and 4.37:1 on input are below the 4.5:1 text threshold but above the 3:1 SC 1.4.11 UI component threshold. Card borders appear against card surfaces, input outlines appear against input surfaces — the borderline cases are on their matching surfaces. Sidebar and background pass 4.5:1.
borders.default.note: v4: borders.default remains hot pink (#FF2070). With text.secondary now electric blue and shadow.md now blue-dominant, borders.default is the anchor that keeps hot pink structurally present. Card borders remain the largest single-area pink element — intentional. The Outrun identity requires hot pink to be unmistakably present at scale.

borders.subtle.wcag: Exempt — decorative structural separator, not text or interactive control.
borders.subtle.note: v5 change: #2A2438 (near-invisible purple) → #112828 (near-invisible dark teal). Still effectively invisible at 1.02:1–1.17:1 — this is a surface separator, not a legibility element. The shift from purple-family to teal-family aligns subtle borders with the teal accent tier rather than implying an unrelated purple layer.

borders.strong.wcag: AA — all surfaces pass with very high margin
borders.strong.note: Night Drive teal. Unchanged from v3.

interactive.focusRing.ratios: Same as borders.strong — 9.91:1 worst case (on input). AA PASS.
interactive.focusRing.note: Teal focus ring is clearly visible on all surfaces.

interactive.hover.note: v4 change: hover shifts from purple-tinted #2A2640 (luminance 0.02249) to blue-tinted #1C2840 (luminance 0.02135). Both are near-identical in lift intensity — the change is hue, not brightness. Blue-tinted hover is consistent with the electric blue accent tier and the dark blue sidebar/card surfaces.

interactive.active.note: v5 change: #33305A (purple-tinted, luminance 0.03556) → #122E2C (teal-tinted, luminance 0.02620). Three-step interaction surface sequence is now: rest (surface bg) → hover #1C2840 (blue-tinted) → active #122E2C (teal-tinted). Each state carries a distinct hue from the three-accent-tier language — blue for transient hover, teal for committed press. No contrast requirement applies — this is a surface token, not a text token.

semantic.error.fix: #119/#120 — split token. error brightened #FF4040 → #FF5050 for text-on-dark use; error-bg added #CC2C2C for bg-error button surfaces (white text on top). Pure neon red family — vivid, maximalist, coherent with Outrun's palette.
semantic.error.wcag: AA — semantic.error passes 4.5:1 as text on all surfaces including hover. semantic.error-bg passes 4.5:1 white text on top.
semantic.error.note: error-bg #CC2C2C is a dark vivid red — retains the pure-red Outrun character without orange or warm tones, just darkened to pass white text. Consistent with Outrun's pure-hue neon approach.

semantic.info.wcag: AA — all surfaces pass. 4.50:1 on input meets AA exactly (unchanged from v3).
semantic.info.note: Thinnest margin in the audit. If semantic.info is ever needed as body text on the input surface, bump to #D060FF (5.22:1 worst case). For current use (streaming indicator), 4.50:1 is sufficient.

_design_notes.revision: Issue #96 v5 — teal amplification: four targeted changes make teal genuinely co-equal with electric blue in the interaction and shadow layers.
_design_notes.v5_changes: Four changes from v4: (1) interactive.active: #33305A (purple) → #122E2C (teal-tinted dark surface). The hover→active state machine is now hue-distinct across the blue and teal accent tiers — hover is blue-tinted (#1C2840), active is teal-tinted (#122E2C); purple is fully retired from the interaction layer. (2) shadow.sm: pink+blue dual-layer → pink+blue+teal three-layer — rgba(255,32,112,0.30) / rgba(61,200,255,0.20) / rgba(46,228,185,0.20). Teal now appears at every elevation tier. Blue opacity reduced from 0.25 to 0.20 to make room. (3) shadow.md: all-blue → blue-dominant with teal secondary — rgba(61,200,255,0.30) + rgba(46,228,185,0.25) + black depth. Blue opacity reduced from 0.35 to 0.30; teal at 0.25 is a genuine secondary presence, not a trace. (4) borders.subtle: #2A2438 (near-invisible purple) → #112828 (near-invisible dark teal). Decorative separator family shifts from purple to teal — still effectively invisible, but teal-family rather than an unrelated purple.
_design_notes.tertiary_blue: #3DC8FF — electric sky blue. Distinctly bluer than the teal family (#2EE4B9, #6BBFB8): teal has significant green content, electric blue does not. Slightly warmer than pure deep sky (#00BFFF) — the +61 red channel keeps it from reading as flat cyan and grounds it in the Outrun neon palette. Passes 4.5:1 on all four surfaces with strong margins (worst case: 8.37:1 on input). text.inverse on tertiary blue: 9.83:1. No schema changes required — tertiary blue is a value assigned to existing tokens.
_design_notes.v4_redistribution: Four changes redistribute color away from pink dominance: (1) text.secondary: #FF6EB4 → #3DC8FF — model names and thread titles shift from pink to electric blue. Highest-impact single move: the most frequently recurring colored text elements now carry the tertiary blue. (2) shadow.md: pink-primary glow → blue-primary glow (rgba 61,200,255,0.35 + black depth). Mid-elevation elements lift with an electric blue corona. (3) shadow.sm: pink+teal dual glow → pink+blue dual glow. Low-elevation lift carries blue secondary layer (0.25 opacity) with pink primary (0.30). (4) interactive.hover: purple-tinted #2A2640 → blue-tinted #1C2840. Hover states carry the tertiary blue at the surface level.
_design_notes.what_stays_pink: borders.default (#FF2070) — hot pink card borders remain the structural anchor. Card borders are the largest-area single-color chromatic element in the UI. Hot pink here is non-negotiable for Outrun character: every card in the interface has a hot pink edge. This single element ensures pink is unmistakably present at scale. The balance shift comes from redistributing where blue appears, not from removing pink from borders.
_design_notes.three_accent_tiers: v5 maintains three clearly distinct chromatic tiers, with teal now genuinely co-equal with blue: (1) HOT PINK (#FF2070, #FF6090) — structural borders (largest area) and Mistral model identity; (2) ELECTRIC BLUE (#3DC8FF) — text.secondary, shadow.sm secondary layer, shadow.md primary glow, interactive.hover tint; (3) TEAL (#2EE4B9, #6BBFB8, #122E2C, #112828) — borders.strong, focusRing, model-gpt, text.muted, interactive.active surface, borders.subtle family, shadow.sm tertiary layer, shadow.md secondary layer. Pink is structural chrome, blue is textual identity, teal is now the interaction and feedback tier — appearing in every shadow level and both interactive states.
_design_notes.shadow_philosophy: v5 shadow system: shadow.sm is low-elevation lift — pink primary (0.30) + blue secondary (0.20) + teal tertiary (0.20). Three-color neon bloom at every elevation. shadow.md is mid-elevation glow — blue primary (0.30) + teal secondary (0.25) + black depth. Blue drops from 0.35 to 0.30 to make room for teal at 0.25 — teal is a genuine second color, not a trace. The two-color blue+teal corona reads as electric water. shadow.lg is unchanged — pink (0.35) + blue (0.25) + teal (0.15) + black depth. High-elevation elements retain the full three-way proportional cascade.
_design_notes.hover_active_logic: Three-step hue-distinct interaction state machine: rest (surface bg) → hover #1C2840 (blue-tinted, luminance 0.02135, transient) → active #122E2C (teal-tinted, luminance 0.02620, committed). v4 introduced blue hover. v5 makes active teal, retiring purple from the interaction layer entirely. Distinguishing signal is hue, not brightness — both hover and active are near-identical in luminance, so the color shift is what communicates the state change.
_design_notes.surface_philosophy: Unchanged from v3. sidebar (#0E1220) and card (#12203A) are dark blue. background (#16141D) and input (#221E34) remain dark purple-near-black. Progressive luminance: sidebar (0.00630) < background (0.00760) < card (0.01467) < input (0.01517).
_design_notes.night_drive_treatment: Unchanged from v3. The dark-purple-near-black content zone and the hot-pink/teal pairing remain the essential Night Drive mood anchors. v4 added electric blue as a distinct third accent. v5 amplifies teal to co-equal standing without adding new hues — the three-tier structure is the same, teal's surface area is expanded.
